title: tragopogon leucanthus
intro: Tragopogon leucanthus, also known as Jack-by-the-hedge or Cocklebur, is a biennial plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic compounds. The health benefits of Tragopogon leucanthus include relief from stomach ulcers, diarrhea, and menstrual cramps, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Tragopogon leucanthus can be found in various forms, including tinctures, infusions, and dried root powders, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
